$comment: C23 <stdbit.h> (N3096 §7.18) — FC17.9(b) (D-FULLC-STDBIT). The type-generic bit utilities. This is the ONLY user-facing surface: the `stdc_*` names appear only on `#include <stdbit.h>` (function-like macros spliced by the preprocessor), while the underlying `__builtin_stdc_<op>_<T>` intrinsics are always-injected compiler builtins (c-subset.lang.json builtinFunctions, reserved `__builtin_` namespace) that lower via a width-correct branchless HIR→MIR composition of the 3 hardware bit-count primitives (Popcount/Clz/Ctz) — NO linkable symbol, so NO `library`/`symbols`, available on every object format. For each of the 14 operations: 4 CONCRETE width macros `stdc_<op>_uc/_us/_ui/_ull(x)` route to the matching-width builtin; `stdc_<op>_ul(x)` is a per-FORMAT `variants` macro carrying the LP64/LLP64 `unsigned long` divergence (pe/LLP64 → the 32-bit `_ui`; elf+macho/LP64 → the 64-bit `_ull`); the GENERIC `stdc_<op>(x)` is a 5-WAY `_Generic` over {unsigned char, unsigned short, unsigned int, unsigned long, unsigned long long} — full C23 §7.18 conformance. The `unsigned long` association routes through the per-format `stdc_<op>_ul` macro above it, so the LP64/LLP64 width divergence stays in exactly ONE place. (It was a 4-WAY with `unsigned long` DROPPED until D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY: DSS derived type IDENTITY from REPRESENTATION, so `unsigned long` interned to a width-core TypeId that COLLIDED with `unsigned int` on LLP64 / `unsigned long long` on LP64 and made a 5-way S_GenericSelectionAmbiguous. Identity now comes from the language vocabulary entry and representation from the target core, so the five associations are five DISTINCT types on every data model.) Each `_Generic` association carries the FULL call (never a bare callee) so only the selected arm lowers (the firstNameToken Area-5 mitigation). Plus the 4 object macros: `__STDC_VERSION_STDBIT_H__` and the endianness triple (all DSS targets are little-endian → NATIVE==LITTLE; a big-endian target is D-FULLC-STDBIT-BIG-ENDIAN-NATIVE). The concrete `stdc_*_T` are macros, so `&stdc_leading_zeros_ui` is D-FULLC-STDBIT-ADDRESSABLE-FN (deferred).

macros.name: stdc_leading_zeros
macros.replacement: _Generic((x), unsigned char: __builtin_stdc_leading_zeros_uc(x), unsigned short: __builtin_stdc_leading_zeros_us(x), unsigned int: __builtin_stdc_leading_zeros_ui(x), unsigned long: stdc_leading_zeros_ul(x), unsigned long long: __builtin_stdc_leading_zeros_ull(x))

macros.name: stdc_leading_ones
macros.replacement: _Generic((x), unsigned char: __builtin_stdc_leading_ones_uc(x), unsigned short: __builtin_stdc_leading_ones_us(x), unsigned int: __builtin_stdc_leading_ones_ui(x), unsigned long: stdc_leading_ones_ul(x), unsigned long long: __builtin_stdc_leading_ones_ull(x))
